build_check.description: Headless launch exits 0 without fatal errors; this 0/1 gate multiplies the rest.

requirements.description: Ships move on a hex-grid sea with movement cost affected by wind direction. A wind indicator shows current direction. Moving downwind costs fewer movement points than moving upwind. 0 if there is no wind mechanic, if movement cost is uniform regardless of direction, or if the grid is not hex-based. Full credit needs authored sprites/illustrated assets; shape/default-widget versions cap at 0.5.

Ships fire broadsides at enemies in their flank arc. Damage depends on range and facing angle. Ships must be oriented correctly to attack — facing matters as much as adjacency. 0 if combat ignores facing, if ships can fire in all directions equally, or if there is no ranged combat. Full credit needs authored sprites/illustrated assets; shape/default-widget versions cap at 0.5.

Treasure islands are captured by moving a ship adjacent and holding position. Controlling islands earns victory points shown in a score tracker. The scenario ends when a point target is reached. 0 if there are no objectives, if islands cannot be captured, or if there is no victory condition. Full credit needs authored sprites/illustrated assets; shape/default-widget versions cap at 0.5.

Ships have HP that decreases from enemy fire. A ship at 0 HP sinks with a visible effect. Sunk ships are permanently lost for the scenario. 0 if ships cannot be damaged, if sinking has no visual feedback, or if ships respawn. Full credit needs authored sprites/illustrated assets; shape/default-widget versions cap at 0.5.

The full loop connects: title -> scenario select -> naval battle (move/fire turns) -> victory/defeat -> result screen -> next scenario or menu. Wind shifts periodically during battle. 0 if any transition is missing or if wind never changes. Full credit needs authored sprites/illustrated assets; shape/default-widget versions cap at 0.5.

At least three ship types exist (sloop, galleon, frigate) with different sprites, movement speeds, HP, firepower, and special abilities (boarding, scouting). 0 if only one ship type exists or if types are functionally identical. Full credit needs distinct authored art; shape/color/text-only variants cap at 0.5.

The map contains islands, shallows (impassable or damaging), and open sea with different tactical implications. At least two map hazard types exist. 0 if the sea is uniform with no terrain features. Full credit needs distinct authored art; shape/color/text-only variants cap at 0.5.

The AI opponent maneuvers ships tactically: positioning for broadside shots, using wind advantage, protecting damaged ships, and contesting treasure islands. 0 if the AI never moves or attacks randomly. Full credit needs distinct authored art; shape/color/text-only variants cap at 0.5.

Boarding actions allow frigates to capture enemy ships rather than sink them, adding them to the player's fleet. Captured ships are usable in subsequent turns. 0 if boarding does not exist or if captured ships disappear. Full credit needs distinct authored art; shape/color/text-only variants cap at 0.5.

Multiple scenarios or campaign missions exist with different map layouts, fleet compositions, and objectives. At least three distinct scenarios are playable. 0 if only one scenario exists. Full credit needs distinct authored art; shape/color/text-only variants cap at 0.5.

The HUD shows wind direction, victory points for both sides, selected ship info (type, HP, movement remaining), and turn indicator in clear non-overlapping regions. 0 if wind, points, or ship info is missing. Full credit needs themed UI with real assets; plain text/unstyled/raw-shape readability caps at 0.5.

The hex sea is clearly rendered with water tiles, islands, shallows, and ship positions. Player and enemy ships are distinguishable. Movement range and broadside arcs are shown when selecting a ship. 0 if hexes are invisible, if ships cannot be told apart, or if arcs are not shown. Full credit needs themed UI with real assets; plain text/unstyled/raw-shape readability caps at 0.5.

Actions produce visible feedback: moving animates the ship across hexes, firing shows cannon smoke and projectiles, hits show damage effects, sinking shows the ship going down, and wind shifts are announced. 0 if actions produce no observable change. Full credit needs themed UI with real assets; plain text/unstyled/raw-shape readability caps at 0.5.

Overall art coherence: sea, ships, islands, cannon effects, UI, and result screens belong to a consistent golden-age piracy visual language with warm nautical tones. 0 if the screen mixes incompatible styles or reads as plain default Godot grey. Programmatic shapes/solid fills/untextured geometry cap at 0.5.

Ship sprites are real illustrated or pixel-art sailing vessels with visible sails, hulls, and cannons. Different ship types have distinct sizes and silhouettes. 0 if ships are plain triangles or colored shapes. Programmatic shapes/solid fills/untextured geometry cap at 0.5.

The sea uses real textured water tiles with wave detail. Islands have illustrated terrain (palm trees, beaches, treasure markers). Shallows are visually distinct from deep water. 0 if the sea is a uniform blue fill. Programmatic shapes/solid fills/untextured geometry cap at 0.5.

HUD and UI use styled themed widgets with nautical chrome: compass rose for wind, ship cards with illustrated frames, and menus with weathered-map backgrounds. 0 if UI elements are unstyled Godot defaults. Programmatic shapes/solid fills/untextured geometry cap at 0.5.

Animation and atmosphere: ships rock on waves, sails billow with wind, cannon fire produces smoke and flash, sinking ships animate downward, and the sea has ambient wave motion. 0 if all elements are static. Programmatic shapes/solid fills/untextured geometry cap at 0.5.
